programmes.show_description: The Lost Letters: The police arrest Ian Brady. A horrific tape recording and photos lead to a search on the moors, revealing a string of victims and Myra Hindley as an accomplice. (Ep2/3)
The Trial: In 1966, Brady and Hindley are brought to court. Witnesses describe the trial, and letters reveal the serial killers' prison lives as Britain's longest-serving inmates. (Ep3/3)
Lakeview Hotel: Gordon Ramsay confronts the owner of the Lakeview Hotel on Lake Chelan in Washington. The bedrooms are empty and the restaurant and ice cream parlour are failing.
The epic adventure reality contest continues. The first player is culled, and another heart-pounding hunt begins. Will alliances count for anything in the thrill of the chase? (Ep2)

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George takes inspiration from Japan, to help Rachel and Sarah with their Victorian terrace. And have a young couple taken on too much with a listed 17th-century cottage?
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.
Stacey helps a multigenerational household to create the spacious kitchen diner they've always dreamt of, and finishes off her own Mediterranean haven poolside project. (Ep6/6)
Kirstie and Phil help Niki and Rob, who want a home that has space for a future family, and Georgie and Louis, who hope to cut down their commute by moving to London.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Give Me a Ring Sometime: The first episode of the classic US comedy. Diane and her fiancé Sumner call in at the eponymous Boston bar before setting off for their wedding.
Sam's Women: Sam tries to get a date with a customer, but Diane is scornful about her.
The Tortelli Tort: When Carla attacks an obnoxious troublemaker, he threatens to sue the bar, unless Sam fires her.
The Bird: Robert and Amy convince their families that it would be fun to spend Thanksgiving together, but the day gets off to a bad start.
Jazz Records: When the kids ruin Ray's dictionary, Frank tells them about the time that Ray wrecked his prized jazz record collection.
Debra at the Lodge: Ray begins to resent how much time Debra is spending away from home when she helps to plan a recruitment party at Frank's lodge.
Shutout in Seattle (Part 2/2): In the concluding part of the sixth season finale, will any of the Crane men sort out their romantic problems?
Momma Mia: Niles and Martin get the shock of their lives when they meet Frasier's new date: she's the spitting image of his mother.
Father of the Bride: Frasier and Martin inadvertently offer to pay for Daphne's wedding. Meanwhile, Frasier's meddling drives Daphne crazy.
Radio Wars: An unwilling Frasier becomes the butt of pranks by the radio station's shock jocks.
Leo's: After his son died, Leo's passion for his Italian restaurant in Texas faded, leading to poor food quality and service. Can Gordon re-ignite Leo's love for la dolce vita?
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry picks a pedal car and Simon grabs a yacht. Buying them is easy. Restoring them is not.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: It's summer, and time for the sheep to be sheared. Matt builds a free-range grazing area for the chickens. And will the farm have its first ever honey harvest?
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Nikki and Gordon want a home with great views on the Maltese island of Gozo. Ben Hillman shows them what their £350k can buy. Has he found the Mediterranean dream?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Can Jo and Ken's musical side hustle turn a profit in Spain? Colin faces a difficult decision in Bulgaria. And will Jon and Angela's wedding be a wash out in France?
The new run continues. Martial arts expert Dan hosts the week's final visit in Southport, where he aims to please with his no-nonsense approach to a classic seaside getaway.
The new run continues. In the West Country, Richard serves up British fare, but his tagliatelle leaves one guest tongue-tied. Will it be a sophisticated soiree or dull as dishwater? (Ep4/5)

programmes.show_name: New: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Jasmine Harman sees how Gary and Lynn are getting on in Haute-Vienne. And are Lorna and Brian's Almería villa and its pool still making a splash?
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Kazal and Eleena are ditching Dubai and moving back home to Solihull to be close to family. Can they overcome building blunders to create the ultimate hosting hub?
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. Emma prepares the perfect family Christmas, but her obsession with work and revenge brings danger to her door. (Ep6/8)
A pedestrian is rushed to A&E with multiple life-threatening injuries after a hit-and-run. An acrobat has a damaged wrist. And a 93-year-old has a broken leg.
Heather, who's 65, is rushed to Queen's after collapsing in the park. Long-serving member of the team Deanna speaks of her previous struggles with addiction.
The Keswick Mountain Rescue team are called to help a fallen scrambler with head and neck injuries who's been precariously lodged at the edge of a waterfall for hours. (Ep4/4)
Brick Hotel: Can Gordon convince the owner of the historic Brick Hotel in Newtown, Pennsylvania, to value her loyal workers and entice locals back to this landmark establishment?
The epic adventure reality contest continues. It's dog-eat-underdog as strategies shift and fortunes flip in the forest, and a shock twist puts even the strongest players in peril. (Ep3)
(2021) Wilderness drama with Robyn Wright. After experiencing a personal tragedy, a woman moves to the Rockies in search of isolation. But can she survive there?
Vicky and Carl have brought their retirement plans forward and are now ready to move to Spain. Jean Johansson shows them what their £70k can buy on the southern Costa Blanca.
In Liverpool, Phil Spencer helps James and Mairead find a family home on a £600,000 budget, while Kirstie Allsopp takes first-time buyers Laura and Andy under her wing.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Sam at Eleven: Sam's delighted when an old baseball teammate arrives to film a sports report about him, but his ego's shattered when he's abandoned mid-interview.
Coach's Daughter: Coach can't understand why his daughter puts up with her slick, unscrupulous, offensive fiancé.
Any Friend of Diane's: Diane's dismayed when her old college friend Rebecca turns up at Cheers on the rebound from an unhappy love affair and tries to make a date with Sam.
Slave: Ray and Debra inadvertently leave the twins home alone and are impressed with Ally when she takes care of them, so they offer her lots of other work...
Whose Side Are You On?: After discovering that Debra places bets with the kids on his strange behaviour, Ray's disturbed to find out that his children think he's a 'doof'.
Lateness: Fed up with his wife's constant tardiness, Ray leaves Debra at home, rather than taking her to an awards ceremony.
Everyone's a Critic: Frasier and Niles are at loggerheads again when Niles gets a new job as the art critic for a prestigious magazine.
The Dog That Rocks the Cradle: Martin returns from his friend's funeral upset by how it was conducted. Roz offers Bulldog babysitting work when he's fired by the radio station.
Rivals: Frasier and Niles compete for the attention of the same woman at a party. At least, they think it's the same woman.
A Tsar Is Born: Frasier and Niles are delighted to discover that they may be descended from Russian royalty.
Blake's Place: In the pursuit of social media fame, Blake has neglected the food quality and service at her New Orleans restaurant. Will she listen to her idol Gordon's advice?
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Tiverton, the boys find a moped that brings back memories, and fight over a petrol gauge that Simon wants to upcycle into a clock.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: It's time for the hay harvest - a big job of mowing meadows, drying the grass and baling it up - in order to give the animals food for the winter.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Nat and Dave are ready to buy a holiday home on Lanzarote, a place they visit twice a year. Jean tries to find their perfect property on the island they love so much.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Bulgaria, Colin and Paul give their horseback riding a trial trot. In Spain, have Jo and Ken's business-boosting experiments moved the dial?
The new run continues. Allegations of game playing are rife on payment day, with one pair vastly underpaying everyone. You could cut the tension with a best-value-B&B plaque!
The new run continues. In the West Country, spiritualist Jo plans a divine dinner of international fare. But will pizza profanities and ghostly goings-on ruin her chances? (Ep5/5)

programmes.show_name: New: A Place in the Sun: What
programmes.show_description: Happened Next?: How are Kate and Wayne are getting on in their perfect villa in Murcia? And what has Keith done to his French fancy in Tarn-et-Garonne?
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programmes.show_name: New: Keir Starmer: Where Did It...
programmes.show_description: ...All Go Wrong? Dispatches: Lewis Goodall examines how the Starmer government became so unpopular so quickly, less than two years after the biggest election landslide in UK history.
The Goggleboxers settle in for more of the biggest and best TV. What do they think this time?
Charlotte Church and Nabil Abdulrashid dig into the week's news with Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker.
